operatorNote: Keep the discussion anchored to divisor count instead of label memorization.

when: Learner can attempt factorization but misses whether all prime factors are present.
operatorNote: Check whether the learner verifies primeness before checking the product.

when: Learner can factorize but struggles on application questions.
operatorNote: Choose the transfer subskill by listening for how the learner reuses factorization.

when: Learner restarts from scratch instead of using factorization to reason about divisor count.
operatorNote: Prompt the learner to say what the factorization already tells them before any divisor listing.

when: Learner cannot identify the missing factor needed to make a square number.
operatorNote: Shift attention to odd prime counts rather than trial-and-error multiplication.

when: Learner mixes common-divisor and common-multiple reasoning in transfer tasks.
operatorNote: Make the learner classify the task as overlap or cover before calculating.

when: Learner uses factorization mechanically but cannot explain which part matters for the new question.
operatorNote: Ask the learner to point to the relevant factor or exponent before solving.
